summary: Partially decompiled the fighter material module by adding `src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c`, moving most of `asm/melee/ft/ftmaterial.s` text into C/inline-asm guarded implementations, and renaming the old anonymous `ft_800BF*`/`ftCo_800BFD*` entry points to `ftMaterial_800BF*`. The PR fully matched `ftMaterial_800BFD04` and `ftMaterial_800BFD9C`, left several larger material/TEV setup routines partially matched, and added the header/type cleanup needed for HSD material, TExp, GObj, fighter bitfields, and GXColor usage.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c` with C/inline-asm implementations for the `ftMaterial_800BF260` through `ftMaterial_800BFD9C` range.
Reduced `asm/melee/ft/ftmaterial.s` from a large text assembly file to mostly remaining data/sdata, including the `ftMObj` class object pointing at `ftMaterial_800BF260`.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` while keeping the assembly object for remaining non-C material data.
Renamed callsites from generic or misplaced names such as `ft_800BFD9C`, `ftCo_800BFD04`, and `ftCo_800BFB4C` to the `ftMaterial_` namespace.
Updated `ftmaterial.h` from placeholder `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations to typed declarations using `HSD_MObj`, `HSD_TExp`, `ftCo_GObj`, and `GXColor`.

Typed several dependencies needed by the new C: `HSD_MObjInfo` now exposes `HSD_MObjSetupFunc setup`, `make_texp`, `setup_tev`, and other material callbacks; `HSD_TExp` became a forward-declared union; `tobj_shadows`, `tobj_toon`, and `HSD_GObj_804D7814` were exposed in headers.

Expanded `Fighter` bitfields by replacing `UnkFlagStruct x2223_flag` and `x2227_flag` with explicit `x2223_b0` through `x2223_b7` and `x2227_b0` through `x2227_b7` members, then updated many users to direct bitfield names.
Added or refined related prototypes including `ftCo_8009F75C(Fighter* fp, bool arg1)` and `gm_80167320(int slot, bool)` to support typed ftmaterial code.

The PR report shows the file at 45.25% matched overall, with `ftMaterial_800BFD04` and `ftMaterial_800BFD9C` at 100%, `ftMaterial_800BF6BC` and `ftMaterial_800BF534` at 0%, and other routines partially matched.
smart_moves: Renaming the whole address range into `ftMaterial_` made the module boundary clearer and cleaned up misleading `ftCo_` names for material setup/death material transitions.
The author did not force all large TEV/material routines to match at once; instead, the PR landed a partial module with typed scaffolding and two fully matched leaf routines.

Keeping MUST_MATCH/inline-asm fallbacks in `ftmaterial.c` preserved build correctness while allowing readable C attempts for complex routines such as `ftMaterial_800BF2B8`, `ftMaterial_800BF534`, and `ftMaterial_800BF6BC`.

Replacing generic flag-wrapper fields with explicit fighter bitfields let the C express render/material conditions like `x2223_b4`, `x2223_b7`, `x2226_b5`, and `x2227_b3` directly, which is useful for future matching and cross-references.

The HSD header work was done alongside the decomp instead of using placeholders everywhere, giving future rendering decomp work reusable types for `HSD_MObjInfo`, `HSD_TExp`, material colors, PE descriptors, toon textures, and shadow textures.
decomp_lessons: Material/rendering code often requires broad header cleanup before meaningful C can be written; here, baselib forward declarations, `mobj.h`, `texp.h`, and fighter structs all had to be improved to support one ftmaterial module.
When a module owns a symbol range, rename all known callsites immediately so future searches find the decompiled module rather than stale address-only names.
Explicit bitfields can be preferable to generic `UnkFlagStruct.bits.bN` when a decompiled routine reads many individual status/render flags and the exact byte/bit positions are known.

For partially matched files, a useful landing point is to add typed function signatures and object-file integration first, then leave larger routines under guarded asm until later matching work can improve them.
Header ownership matters: globals such as `HSD_GObj_804D7814` should live in public headers when C code needs them, rather than being repeated as local externs in `.c` files.
naming_conventions: The module uses address-preserving names of the form `ftMaterial_800Bxxxx` until semantic names are known.
Fighter flags were normalized from `fp->x2223_flag.bits.bN` and `fp->x2227_flag.bits.bN` to direct names like `fp->x2223_b7` and `fp->x2227_b4`.
Function prototypes in headers use address comments such as `/* 0BF2B8 */`, matching existing project convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The new C file uses `ASM` functions with `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` readable C bodies and `#else` inline assembly fallbacks, allowing partial progress without losing exact assembly behavior.
`asm/melee/ft/ftmaterial.s` retained data/sdata such as `ftMObj`, `ftCo_804D3C00`, and `ftCo_804D3C08` while text moved to the C object.
The build was adjusted to compile both `src/melee/ft/ftmaterial.c.o` and the remaining `asm/melee/ft/ftmaterial.s.o`, a common migration pattern for mixed C/data assembly files.
The diff preserves exact branch/call targets in assembly callsites while updating symbol names, e.g. death animation asm now branches to `ftMaterial_800BFD9C` and `ftMaterial_800BFD04`.

The human PR report gives concrete matching status: `ftMaterial_800BFD04` and `ftMaterial_800BFD9C` matched 100%, while `ftMaterial_800BF260`, `ftMaterial_800BF2B8`, and `ftMaterial_800BFB4C` remained partial.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there is no reviewer text, no reviewer intent or requested follow-up can be inferred from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve match percentages for `ftMaterial_800BF2B8`, `ftMaterial_800BF534`, `ftMaterial_800BF6BC`, or `ftMaterial_800BFB4C`.
Search for subsequent semantic renames of `ftMaterial_800BFD04` and `ftMaterial_800BFD9C`.
Check later changes to `HSD_MObjInfo`, `HSD_TExp`, `HSD_PEDesc`, `tobj_shadows`, and `tobj_toon` for reusable baselib rendering type refinements.
Look for PRs that replace `M2C_FIELD` or inline-asm fallbacks in `ftmaterial.c` with fully typed C.
Search for additional fighter flag cleanup around `x2223_b*`, `x2226_b5`, and `x2227_b*` to learn their eventual semantic names.
